properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued May 31 at 10:41PM CDT until May 31 at 11:00PM CDT by NWS Lubbock TX
properties.description:
At 1041 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated rain was ending over the
warned area. Earlier heavy rainfall of between 1 and 3 inches
resulted in widespread flooding, including the closure of Highway 87
about 5 miles north of Tahoka. Flooding impacts will continue, but
little to no additional rainfall is expected. Continue to heed road
closures tonight.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar and law enforcement.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Wilson, and portions of Highway 87 north of Tahoka.
properties.instruction:
Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

NWSheadline: FLASH FLOOD WARNING WILL BE ALLOWED TO EXPIRE AT 11 PM CDT THIS EVENING FOR NORTHEASTERN LYNN COUNTY

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued May 31 at 10:34PM CDT until June 1 at 12:00AM CDT by NWS Lubbock TX
properties.description:
At 1034 PM CDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges indicated
slow-moving thunderstorms producing moderate rain across the warned
area. Up to 1.7 inches of rain have fallen near 34th Street and
Upland Avenue in west Lubbock. Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5
inch are possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar and automated gauges.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Lubbock, Wolfforth, Reese Center and Lubbock South Plains Mall.
properties.instruction:
Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

NWSheadline: FLASH FLOOD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL MIDNIGHT CDT TONIGHT FOR WEST CENTRAL LUBBOCK COUNTY
